seoTitle: How to Make a Portfolio on Google Docs
metaDescription: Learn how to build a reusable Google Docs template for a portfolio or standardized document, complete with placeholders and a shareable copy link.
intro: Building a portfolio or any document you'll reuse repeatedly is much easier once you understand Google Docs' template workflow. Instead of copy-pasting old files, you can design a master layout with placeholder text, store it in Drive, and share a link that automatically prompts others to make their own copy. This guide walks through that setup.

steps.heading: Build your master layout
steps.body: Structure your base document with headings, sections, and placeholder text or smart chips marking where content should go.

steps.heading: Set baseline sharing
steps.body: Go to File > Share and confirm view-only access so the master file itself stays protected from accidental edits.

steps.heading: Submit to your template gallery
steps.body: From the Google Docs home screen, open the Template Gallery, select your organization's tab, and click 'Submit Template.'

steps.heading: Select and categorize your file
steps.body: Choose your master document from Drive, assign it an accurate category, and click 'Submit.'

steps.heading: Force a copy with a URL trick
steps.body: Alternatively, share a link with 'edit' replaced by 'copy' in the URL so anyone opening it is prompted to duplicate the file instead of editing the original.

faq.question: Can I make a portfolio template without submitting it to a gallery?
faq.answer: Yes, using the '/copy' URL trick lets you share a forced-copy link without needing the Template Gallery at all.

faq.question: Will edits by others affect my master file?
faq.answer: No, as long as you keep the master file's sharing permissions restricted or share the copy-forcing link instead.

faq.question: Can I use smart chips as placeholders?
faq.answer: Yes, Google Docs' smart chips can mark data fields that are easy to spot and replace when someone copies the template.

faq.question: Is the Template Gallery available on all Google accounts?
faq.answer: Personal accounts have access to a general gallery, while organization-specific submission may depend on your Workspace admin settings.
